## Change Log

| Version | Change | Reference |
|---|---|---|
| 0.1.0 | Initial single-cylinder Gurney–Mott field model. | — |
| 0.2.0 | Add $A_p(\gamma, \text{posture})$ replacement for the width-based geometry factor (eq. 22'). | `updates/target-area-profile/derivation.md` |
| 0.3.0 | Four-zone (ogive/cylinder/boattail/base) decomposition with zone-local Gurney, Mott, and spray angles; AoF ground projection; $A_p$-based geometry factor integrated. | `updates/frag-field-3d-geometry/derivation.md` |
| 0.3.1 | Refactor: all physics moved to `src/arty/` (`fragmentation.py`, new `zones.py`, new `plots.py`); notebook split into per-section partials. Behaviour-preserving — no value changes. | — |
| 0.4.0 | Add target-independent lethal-fragment density field $\rho_L(x,y,z)$ (eq. 3) for both single-zone and four-zone paths: divides out the presented-area $A_p$ and graded $P_k\|\text{hit}$ factors, counting only fragments with arrival KE $\ge E_\text{leth} = 1000$ J (ES-310 $P_k\|\text{hit}=0.5$ anchor). | `updates/lethal-fragment-density-field/derivation.md` |

______________________________________________________________________

## Key Findings

```{python}
from dataclasses import replace

print("=" * 58)
print("  105 mm M1 HE — Fragmentation Field Summary")
print("=" * 58)
print(f"  V₀  (Gurney)          : {V0:.0f} m/s")
print(f"  μ   (half-weight)     : {mu*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  2μ  (avg frag mass)   : {2*mu*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  μ/4 (mode mass)       : {mu/4*1e3:.2f} g")
print(f"  N₀  (total frags)     : {N0:.0f}")
print(f"  N(m > 1 g)            : {mott_N(np.array([1e-3]), N0, mu)[0]:.0f}")
print(f"  N(m > 10 g)           : {mott_N(np.array([10e-3]), N0, mu)[0]:.0f}")
print(f"  R₅₀ (standing, ES-310): {R50:.0f} m")

print("\n  γ sensitivity (σ_F = 800 MPa fixed):")
for g in [40, 53, 65, 80, 100]:
    shell_g = replace(shell, steel=replace(shell.steel, gamma=float(g)))
    mu_g, _ = mott_params(shell_g, V0)
    N0_g  = mass_shell / (2*mu_g)
    Ne_g  = expected_kills(r_vals, N0_g, mu_g, V0, drag, rho_steel, w_target)
    r50_g = r_vals[np.argmin(np.abs(p_kill(Ne_g) - 0.5))]
    n05_g = mott_N(np.array([0.5e-3]), N0_g, mu_g)[0]
    print(f"    γ={g:>3d}  N₀={N0_g:>5.0f}  N(>0.5g)={n05_g:>5.0f}  R₅₀={r50_g:.0f} m")

print("\n  σ_F sensitivity (γ = 65 fixed):")
for sf_mpa in [600, 700, 800, 900, 1000]:
    shell_s = replace(shell, steel=replace(shell.steel, sigma_f=sf_mpa*1e6))
    mu_s, _ = mott_params(shell_s, V0)
    N0_s  = mass_shell / (2*mu_s)
    Ne_s  = expected_kills(r_vals, N0_s, mu_s, V0, drag, rho_steel, w_target)
    r50_s = r_vals[np.argmin(np.abs(p_kill(Ne_s) - 0.5))]
    n05_s = mott_N(np.array([0.5e-3]), N0_s, mu_s)[0]
    print(f"    σ_F={sf_mpa} MPa  N₀={N0_s:>5.0f}  N(>0.5g)={n05_s:>5.0f}  R₅₀={r50_s:.0f} m")
```

**Parameter sensitivity notes:**

- **γ** is the dominant uncertainty. For SAE 1045 steel (M1 shell), Mott's 1947
  table brackets it at 53–67; the R₅₀ varies by ±10–15 m across this range.
- **σ_F** (dynamic flow stress) is the second uncertainty; 800–1 000 MPa is the
  high-strain-rate range for hardened steel. Higher σ_F produces larger average
  fragments and a slightly larger lethal radius.
- **Angle of fall** is modelled in the four-zone field (§6). At AoF ≈ 20°–35°
  (WW2 105 mm fire at 4–6 km), the forward sector receives more ogive-zone
  fragments while the rear sector receives base-zone fragments; the symmetric
  disk (eq. 9) is superseded by the asymmetric `four_zone_field` result.
- The ES-310 Pk|hit curve is calibrated for standing unprotected infantry. Against a prone
  target the presented area halves and the effective lethal radius shrinks ~30%.

______________________________________________________________________
